Careers

Executive Director

In 2026, FoundHer became an independent 501(c)(3). This is a founding moment, and weʼre looking for the leader who will carry it forward for generations of wāhine founders.

{{ t.k }} {{ t.v }}

Reports to the Hawaiʻi FoundHer Board of Directors

The Role

The Executive Director is responsible for the overall leadership, operations, and sustainability of Hawaiʻi FoundHer. The majority of the time will be spent fundraising and building community partnerships.

Reporting to the Board of Directors, the ED ensures the organization enacts its mission, manages day-to-day program operations, builds and maintains a strong funding base, and serves as FoundHerʼs primary external voice.

This is a hands-on leadership role. FoundHer operates with a small, dedicated team, which means the ED is both strategic leader and active implementer.

Who Weʼre Looking For

FoundHer is a culturally grounded organization. The right candidate doesnʼt just understand this mission intellectually, they embody it.

They operate with humility, center community over institution, and lead by bringing people in rather than directing from above. They are comfortable in a boardroom and in a community gathering. They take data seriously and use it to tell honest stories about impact.

If you come from outside the Native Hawaiian community, we expect you to name that honestly, lead with hāʻahāʻ, and demonstrate a track record of being accountable to and learning from indigenous communities.

This is a role for someone who is energized, not overwhelmed, by the challenge of building something lasting.

Key Responsibilities

Four areas of leadership.

Ready to lead FoundHer?

Send your résumé and a note about why this work matters to you. We review applications on a rolling basis.
